reasoning: Rouen has a strong seasonal temperature cycle. The daily average temp in late October is typically around 11.5°C, while late November drops to ~7.0°C and late January drops to ~4.5°C. Since the exact value on Oct 26 is unknown, we find the probability of increased temp by comparing the distributions. Assuming daily average standard deviations of ~4°C, the mean expected difference (Y - X) becomes increasingly negative. For Nov 2 (a week later), the expected drop is ~1°C, yielding ~42% chance of being warmer. For Nov 25, the drop is ~4.5°C (~22% chance). By Jan 24, the expected drop is ~7.0°C (~14% chance). Unlike random walk time series, temperature has predictable seasonality, so probabilities of an increase decline confidently below 0.5 as we move deeper into winter.
